2606.19911.title: Multi-Agent Transactive Memory
2606.19911.paper_summary: The decentralized deployment of LLM agents with diverse capabilities across diverse tasks motivates infrastructure for knowledge sharing across heterogeneous agent populations. Just as search engines index human-generated artifacts to support human problem solving, retrieval systems can organize agent-generated artifacts for reuse across agent populations. We extend retrieval-augmented generation - which demonstrates the value of human-authored artifacts to individual agents - to retrieval of agent-generated artifacts supporting a population of agents. In particular, agent trajectories encode reusable procedural knowledge, yet these artifacts are typically discarded after a single use or retained only by the producing agent, forcing newly instantiated agents to repeatedly rediscover existing solutions. We propose Multi-Agent Transactive Memory (MATM), a framework for population-level storage and retrieval of agent-generated trajectories, where producer agents contribute trajectories to a shared repository and consumer agents retrieve them to improve task execution. We focus on interactive environments (ALFWorld and WebArena), where trajectories are long and encode especially rich procedural structure. Our experiments demonstrate that retrieving trajectories from MATM improves downstream task performance and reduces interaction steps without coordination or joint training. These results position MATM as a design pattern for population-level experience sharing in open agent ecosystems.
2606.19911.paper_summary_zh: 去中心化的 LLM 代理的部署，具備多樣的能力以應對各種任務，促進了異質代理群體之間的知識共享基礎設施。正如搜尋引擎索引人類產生的文物以支持人類解決問題，檢索系統可以組織代理生成的文物，以便在代理群體中重複使用。我們擴展了檢索增強生成——這顯示了人類創作的文物對個別代理的價值——以檢索支持代理群體的代理生成文物。特別是，代理軌跡編碼了可重用的程序知識，但這些文物通常在單次使用後被丟棄，或者僅由生產代理保留，迫使新實例化的代理重複重新發現現有解決方案。我們提出了多代理交易記憶（MATM），這是一個針對代理生成軌跡的群體級存儲和檢索框架，其中生產代理將軌跡貢獻給共享庫，而消費代理則檢索這些軌跡以改善任務執行。我們專注於互動環境（ALFWorld 和 WebArena），在這些環境中，軌跡較長並編碼了特別豐富的程序結構。我們的實驗表明，從 MATM 檢索軌跡改善了下游任務性能，並在沒有協調或聯合訓練的情況下減少了互動步驟。這些結果使 MATM 成為開放代理生態系統中群體級經驗共享的設計模式。
2606.19911.author: To Eun Kim et.al.
2606.19911.authors: To Eun Kim, Xuhong He, Dishank Jain, Ambuj Agrawal, Negar Arabzadeh, Fernando Diaz
